picks.title: Nanocoder as an ACP agent: driving it from Zed
picks.why: Most substantive recent deep-dive at 8 days old, and ACP for Zed is the kind of architectural change that rewards a long-form GitHub Discussion post explaining how the agent surface actually differs from the VS Code WebSocket extension.

Reddit rewards practical wins for self-hosters, and the auto-tune profile story (33 to 19 tools, model-size detection, live re-resolution) is a meaty real-world improvement that small-local-model runners will actually want to read about.
Editor-as-UI for a coding agent is a strong professional-facing narrative for LinkedIn, and this post frames the ACP server as a deliberate step beyond the terminal panel rather than a feature dump.

The strongest 280-character hook in the catalogue right now - 'nanocoder --acp runs headless, editor becomes the UI' lands the whole ACP-for-Zed story in one line with concrete deliverables (streaming, tool cards, diffs, permissions).
